name: Smith Machine Bent-Over Row
resistance_profile: The fixed vertical bar path removes balance demands, letting you chase heavy mid-range back tension in a consistent groove.
cues: Flat back, chest over the bar
Row to the lower ribs, elbows back
Hold the hinge — don't stand up to lift
execution_steps: Set the Smith bar at about mid-shin height and load the weight.
Stand over the bar, hinge to a flat-back bent-over position, and take a shoulder-width overhand grip.
Unrack by rotating the bar, then row it to the lower ribs, driving the elbows up and back.
Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top without standing up.
Lower along the fixed path to a full stretch, then re-rack after the set.
good_when: You want to row heavy without fighting for balance.
You want a fixed groove to focus purely on back contraction.
You are learning bent-over rowing and want extra stability.
bad_when: You want to train real-world stabilizers and free-weight balance.
The fixed vertical path irritates your shoulders or lower back.
common_errors: Using the legs and standing up to heave the bar
Rowing too high toward the chest and shrugging
Rounding the lower back under load
progressions: Add load or reps, or pause each rep at the top.
Slow the eccentric or add a dead-stop on the pins each rep.
regressions: Reduce load or raise the bar start height to shorten the range.
Switch to a chest-supported machine row if the hinge bothers your back.
